ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic croissant entry (FOODON_03315595). No specific entry exists for a flattened, caramelized, red-bean-filled croissant variant. Closest parent match used; filling and preparation specifics are not captured by the ontology code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers generic croissant. The red bean and butter filling, caramelized sugar coating, and flattened crisp preparation are not represented. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific filled croissant variant. SNOMED CT does not provide sufficient granularity for this food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one flat crisp croissant weighing approximately 100 g, consumed as a snack or light meal item.

nutrient_source.title: Red Bean & Butter Flat Crisp Croissant — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (cr_id G00011004). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from butter lamination composition (approximately 11 g/100 g total fat 21 g, consistent with butter-laminated pastry benchmarks from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for croissant, USDA #18023), as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: Croissant, plain — Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a flattened caramelized red bean butter croissant. The GI of 67 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain croissant (GI ~67, medium category), which is the closest defensible analogous food. The red bean and chestnut filling adds low-GI components (red beans GI ~24–29) that may modestly reduce the overall GI, but the dominant refined flour and caramelized sugar matrix is consistent with the plain croissant GI anchor. Glycemic load calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (67 × 45.8) / 100 = 30.7 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium (300 mg/100 g) combined with high sugar (11.26 g/100 g) and low potassium (118 mg/100 g), creating an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio with insufficient fibre offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this item due to borderline-high sodium at 300 mg/100 g, high added sugar from caramelized coating and sweetened red bean paste, and a low potassium content of only 118 mg/100 g that provides minimal vascular offset; the combined sugar and sodium burden is clinically unfavourable for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High available carbohydrate (45.8 g/100 g) from refined T45 flour with medium GI (~67) and 11.26 g/100 g added sugar from caramelized coating and sweetened red bean paste, producing a high glycaemic load of approximately 30.7 per 100 g serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This item is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycaemic load driven by refined flour, caramelized black and brown sugar coating, and sweetened red bean paste; the modest dietary fibre of 2.6 g/100 g is insufficient to meaningfully blunt the postprandial glucose response, and consumption should be avoided or strictly portion-controlled.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 11 g/100 g from butter lamination, combined with 67 mg/100 g cholesterol and 0.1 g/100 g trans fat, presents a clinically significant atherogenic lipid profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this item due to its high estimated saturated fat content of approximately 11 g/100 g from butter lamination, the presence of 0.1 g/100 g trans fat which is pro-atherogenic at any intake level, and cholesterol at 67 mg/100 g; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high glycaemic load creates a compounded cardiovascular risk profile that is inconsistent with heart-protective dietary patterns.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~11 g/100 g from butter lamination exceeds the 5 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold; trans fat at 0.1 g/100 g and refined sugar from sweetened red bean paste further promote inflammatory mediators.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item as the high saturated fat from butter lamination, presence of trans fat, and refined sugar from the caramelized coating and sweetened red bean paste collectively promote systemic inflammation and are inconsistent with an anti-inflammatory dietary approach.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Red Bean and Butter Flat Crisp Croissant is a Singaporean fusion bakery item that reimagines the classic French croissant in a distinctly East Asian style. The croissant dough is flattened and baked to achieve an overall crispy, shatteringly thin texture rather than the traditional layered, airy crumb. The exterior is coated with a blend of black sugar and brown sugar that caramelizes during baking, producing a glossy, amber-toned crust with a pronounced sweet, slightly bitter caramel flavour. The interior is filled with a homemade low-sugar red bean and chestnut paste paired with a block of natural butter, creating a contrast between the sweet, earthy legume filling and the rich, savoury dairy fat. Per 100 g, this item delivers 406 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 21 g fat (estimated 11 g saturated from butter lamination), 45.8 g carbohydrate, 11.26 g sugar, 2.6 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 67 (medium) based on the plain croissant analogue from the University of Sydney GI Database, yielding a high glycaemic load of approximately 30.7 per 100 g serving. Clinically, this item is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high glycaemic load, elevated saturated fat, borderline sodium, and low potassium-to-sodium ratio. The presence of 0.1 g/100 g trans fat adds further cardiovascular and inflammatory risk. Red beans in isolation are a diabetes-suitable, anti-inflammatory legume, but their benefit is substantially offset in this preparation by the sweetened paste, refined flour, and butter content. This item is best consumed occasionally and in small portions by healthy adults.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this specific product (cr_id G00011004) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from butter-laminated pastry benchmarks using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (croissant #18023) as a secondary reference, which slightly reduces certainty for that field. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database plain croissant entry. Overall confidence is rated high given the canonical HPB provenance for the majority of nutrient fields.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (11.0 g/100 g) is estimated from butter lamination composition benchmarks using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy croissant entry (#18023) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. This is the primary uncertainty in the nutrient profile. 2. Trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is carried forward from the input health_suitability notes as a plausible estimate for a butter-laminated pastry; this value is not explicitly reported in the HPB record and should be verified against the product label. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this specific product is not reported in the HPB database as of 2026-04-16. 5. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product as of 2026-04-16. 6. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database plain croissant entry; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific filled and caramelized variant. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; the product contains butter and the halal certification status of the source bakery is unknown. 8. The food_name has been normalized from 'Red Bean & Butter Flat Crisp Croissant' to 'Red Bean and Butter Flat Crisp Croissant' for canonical English naming convention.
